source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8181_Passage 3 Extinct The Giant Deer.html

passages.title: Extinct: The Giant Deer
passages.source_html:
Extinct: The Giant Deer 


Toothed cats, mastodons, giant sloths, woolly rhinos, and many other big, shaggy mammals are widely thought to have died out around the end of the last ice age, some 10,500 years ago.


A 


The Irish elk is also known as the giant deer (Megaloceros giganteus). Analysis of ancient bones and teeth by scientists based in Britain and Russia show the huge herbivore survived until about 5,000 B.C. – more than three millennia later than previously believed. The research team says this suggests additional factors, besides climate change, probably hastened the giant deer’s eventual extinction. The factors could include hunting or habitat destruction by humans. 


B 


The Irish elk, so-called because its well-preserved remains are often found in lake sediments under peat bogs in Ireland, first appeared about 400,000 years ago in Europe and Central Asia. Through a combination of radiocarbon dating of skeletal remains and the mapping of locations where the remains were unearthed, the team shows the Irish elk was widespread across Europe before the last ‘big freeze.’ The deer’s range later contracted to the Ural Mountains, in modern-day Russia, which separate Europe from Asia. 


C 


The giant deer made its last stand in western Siberia, some 3,000 years after the ice sheets receded, said the study’s co-author, Adrian Lister, professor of palaeobiology at University College London, England. ‘The eastern foothills of the Urals became very densely forested about 8,000 years ago, which could have pushed them on to the plain,’ he said. He added that pollen analysis indicates the region then became very dry in response to further climatic change, leading to the loss of important food plants. ‘In combination with human pressures, this could have finally snuffed them out,’ Lister said. 


D 


Hunting by humans has often been put forward as a contributory cause of extinction of the Pleistocene megafauna. The team, though, said their new date for the Irish elk’s extinction hints at an additional human-made problem – habitat destruction. Lister said, ‘We haven’t got just hunting 7,000 years ago – this was also about the time the first Neolithic people settled in the region. They were farmers who would have cleared the land.’ The presence of humans may help explain why the Irish elk was unable to tough out the latest of many climatic fluctuations – periods it had survived in the past. 


E 


Meanwhile, Lister cast doubt on another possible explanation for the deer’s demise – the male’s huge antlers. Some scientists have suggested this exaggerated feature – the result of females preferring stags with the largest antlers, possibly because they advertised a male’s fitness – contributed to the mammal’s downfall. They say such antlers would have been a serious inconvenience in the dense forests that spread northward after the last ice age. But, Lister said, ‘That’s a hard argument to make because the deer previously survived perfectly well through wooded interglacials [warmer periods between ice ages].’ Some research has suggested that a lack of sufficient high-quality forage caused the extinction of the elk. High amounts of calcium and phosphate compounds are required to form antlers, and therefore large quantities of these minerals are required for the massive structures of the Irish Elk. The males (and male deer in general) met this requirement partly from their bones, replenishing them from food plants after the antlers were grown or reclaiming the nutrients from discarded antlers (as has been observed in extant deer). Thus, in the antler growth phase, Giant Deer was suffering from a condition similar to osteoporosis. When the climate changed at the end of the last glacial period, the vegetation in the animal’s habitat also changed towards species that presumably could not deliver sufficient amounts of the required minerals, at least in the western part of its range. 


F 


The extinction of megafauna around the world was almost completed by the end of the last ice age. It is believed that megafauna initially came into existence in response to glacial conditions and became extinct with the onset of warmer climates. Tropical and subtropical areas have experienced less radical climatic change. The most dramatic of these changes was the transformation of a vast area of North Africa into the world’s largest desert. Significantly, Africa escaped major faunal extinction as did tropical and sub-tropical Asia. The human exodus from Africa and our entrance into the Americas and Australia were also accompanied by climate change. Australia’s climate changed from cold-dry to warm-dry. As a result, surface water became scarce. Most inland lakes became completely dry or dry in the warmer seasons. Most large, predominantly browsing animals lost their habitat and retreated to a narrow band in eastern Australia, where there were permanent water and better vegetation. Some animals may have survived until about 7,000 years ago. If people have been in Australia for up to 60,000 years, then megafauna must have co-existed with humans for at least 30,000 years. Regularly hunted modern kangaroos survived not only 10,000 years of Aboriginal hunting, but also an onslaught of commercial shooters. 


G 


The group of scientists led by A.J. Stuart focused on northern Eurasia, which he was taking as Europe, plus Siberia, essentially, where they’ve got the best data that animals became extinct in Europe during the Late Pleistocene. Some cold-adapted animals, go through into the last part of the cold stage and then become extinct up there. So you’ve actually got two phases of extinction. Now, neither of these coincide – these are Neanderthals here being replaced by modern humans. There’s no obvious coincidence between the arrival of humans or climatic change alone and these extinctions. There’s a climatic change here, so there’s a double effect here. Again, as animals come through to the last part of the cold stage, here there’s a fundamental change in the climate, reorganization of vegetation, and the combination of the climatic change and the presence of humans – of advanced Paleolithic humans – causes this wave of extinction. There’s a profound difference between the North American data and that of Europe, which summarize that the extinctions in northern Eurasia, in Europe, are moderate and staggered, and in North America severe and sudden. And these things relate to the differences in the timing of human arrival. The extinction follows from human predation, but only at times of fundamental changes in the environment.

groups.title: Questions 28-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 28-32


Complete the summary below. Using NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 28-32 on your answer sheet.


28

Having been preserved well in Europe and Central Asia, the remains of the Irish elk was initially found approximately 28 .

正确答案： 28 400000 years ago##400000 Years Ago
Question 28

原文答案句：The Irish elk, so-called because its well-preserved remains are often found in lake sediments under peat bogs in Ireland, first appeared about 400,000 years ago in Europe and Central Asia.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是爱尔兰麋鹿的遗骸最初被发现的大致时间。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"Europe and Central Asia"和"preserved well"定位到B段第一句，该句提到了爱尔兰麋鹿首次出现的时间。

解题思路：根据原文中"first appeared about 400,000 years ago in Europe and Central Asia"可以确定答案为"400,000 years ago"。


29

Around 29 , they were driven to live in the plain after being restricted to the Ural Mountains. Hunting was considered as one of the important factors of Irish elk’s extinction,

正确答案： 29 8000 years ago##8000 Years Ago
Question 29

原文答案句：‘The eastern foothills of the Urals became very densely forested about 8,000 years ago, which could have pushed them on to the plain,’ he said.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是在什么时间点，巨型鹿被迫从乌拉尔山脉迁移到平原，并且狩猎被认为是导致爱尔兰麋鹿灭绝的重要因素之一。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"Ural Mountains"和"plain"可以定位到C段中描述乌拉尔山脉东部山麓变得非常茂密的句子。

解题思路：根据C段中的句子，可以确定大约在8000年前，乌拉尔山脉的东部山麓变得非常茂密，这可能将巨型鹿推向平原。同时，D段提到狩猎被认为是导致爱尔兰麋鹿灭绝的重要因素之一，因此可以确定答案为"8000 years ago"。


30

people have not started hunting until 30 when Irish elk used to get through under a variety of climatic fluctuations.

正确答案： 30 7000 years ago##7000 Years Ago
Question 30

原文答案句：Lister said, ‘We haven’t got just hunting 7,000 years ago – this was also about the time the first Neolithic people settled in the region. They were farmers who would have cleared the land.’

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是人们在什么时间开始狩猎，而爱尔兰麋鹿过去曾经历过各种气候波动。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"hunting"和"climatic fluctuations"定位到D段，其中提到了狩猎和气候波动。

解题思路：根据D段中的原文句子，可以确定人们在7000年前开始狩猎，而这也是新石器时代人们首次在该地区定居的时间。因此，答案为"7000 years ago"。


31

The huge antlers may possibly contribute to the reason why Irish elk extinct, which was highly controversial as they live pleasantly over the span of 31 .

正确答案： 31 wooded interglacials##Wooded Interglacials
Question 31

原文答案句：But, Lister said, ‘That’s a hard argument to make because the deer previously survived perfectly well through wooded interglacials [warmer periods between ice ages].’ 

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问的是爱尔兰麋鹿在什么样的时期中生存得很好，它们巨大的角是其灭绝原因的这种说法存疑。  

定位关键信息：题目中的"live pleasantly over the span of"对应原文中的"survived perfectly well through"。  

解题思路：原文E段提到，Lister质疑巨大角导致爱尔兰麋鹿灭绝的说法，原因是这种麋鹿之前在森林间冰期"wooded interglacials"（冰期之间的温暖期，且森林茂密）中生存得很好。因此答案为"wooded interglacials"。


32

Generally, it is well-known that, at the last maximum ice age, mammals become extinct about 32 .

正确答案： 32 10500 years##10500 Years
Question 32

原文答案句：Toothed cats, mastodons, giant sloths, woolly rhinos, and many other big, shaggy mammals are widely thought to have died out around the end of the last ice age, some 10,500 years ago.

对应段落：题记

审题：题目问的是在最后一次冰河时代的末期，哺乳动物大约在什么时候灭绝。

定位关键信息：利用题目的单词"last maximum ice age"和"mammals become extinct"来定位原文中的句子。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子"Toothed cats, mastodons, giant sloths, woolly rhinos, and many other big, shaggy mammals are widely thought to have died out around the end of the last ice age, some 10,500 years ago."可以确定，哺乳动物在最后一次冰河时代的末期大约在10,500年前灭绝。因此，答案是10500 years。


Questions 33-35


Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND / OR NUMBERS from the passage for each answer.


33

33 What kind of physical characteristics eventually contributed to the extinction of Irish elk?

正确答案： 33 Male's huge antlers##male's huge antlers
Question 33

原文答案句：Some scientists have suggested this exaggerated feature – the result of females preferring stags with the largest antlers, possibly because they advertised a male’s fitness – contributed to the mammal’s downfall. They say such antlers would have been a serious inconvenience in the dense forests that spread northward after the last ice age.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是爱尔兰麋鹿最终灭绝的物理特征是什么。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"physical characteristics"和"extinction of Irish elk"定位到文章中描述爱尔兰麋鹿灭绝原因的部分。在E段中，作者提到了关于雄性巨大鹿角的讨论。

解题思路：在E段中，作者提到一些科学家认为雄性巨大的鹿角可能是导致爱尔兰麋鹿灭绝的原因。这些鹿角在冰河时代后的茂密森林中可能会造成不便。因此，根据原文信息，可以确定题目答案为"Male's huge antlers"。


34

34 What kind of nutrient substance needed in maintaining the huge size of Irish elk?

正确答案： 34 minerals##Minerals
Question 34

原文答案句：High amounts of calcium and phosphate compounds are required to form antlers, and therefore large quantities of these minerals are required for the massive structures of the Irish Elk.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问爱尔兰麋鹿维持其巨大鹿角所需的营养物质。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"maintaining the huge size of Irish elk"和"nutrient substance"在文章中定位。在E段中找到提到爱尔兰麋鹿巨大体型所需的物质。

解题思路：原文明确提到形成巨大鹿角需要大量的"minerals"（矿物质），并且这些矿物质对于爱尔兰麋鹿的巨大结构是必需的。题目问的是维持巨大体型所需的营养物质，因此直接对应原文中的"minerals"。


35

35 What geographical evidence suggested the advent of human resulted in the extinction of Irish elk?

正确答案： 35 habitat destruction##Habitat Destruction
Question 35

原文答案句：The team, though, said their new date for the Irish elk’s extinction hints at an additional human-made problem – habitat destruction.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是“什么证据表明，人类的到来导致了爱尔兰麋鹿的灭绝？”  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"geographical evidence"、"advent of human"和"extinction of Irish elk"定位到D段，该段提到研究团队认为爱尔兰麋鹿灭绝的新日期暗示了一个额外的人为问题。  

解题思路：原文中明确提到"habitat destruction"（栖息地破坏）是研究团队提出的一个额外人为问题，这与题目中询问的证据相吻合。因此，"habitat destruction"是正确答案。


Questions 36-39


Choose the letter A-D and write your answers in boxes 36-39 on your answer sheet.


A Eurasia 

B Australia 

C Asia 

D Africa 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
36

36 the continents where humans imposed a little impact on large mammals extinction
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Some animals may have survived until about 7,000 years ago. If people have been in Australia for up to 60,000 years, then megafauna must have co-existed with humans for at least 30,000 years. Regularly hunted modern kangaroos survived not only 10,000 years of Aboriginal hunting, but also an onslaught of commercial shooters. 

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“人类对大型哺乳动物灭绝影响较小的大洲”，需要找出文章中提到人类影响较小或几乎没有影响的地区。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“humans imposed a little impact on large mammals extinction”可以定位到F段中提到非洲和热带亚洲没有发生大规模动物灭绝，而澳大利亚虽然经历了气候变化，但大型动物与人类共存了很长时间，人类的影响相对较小。

解题思路：原文中F段后半部分描述了澳大利亚的情况，虽然气候发生了变化，但大型动物与人类共存了很长时间，人类的影响相对较小。因此，澳大利亚是人类对大型哺乳动物灭绝影响较小的洲。
37

37 the continents where the climatic change was mild and fauna remains
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Significantly, Africa escaped major faunal extinction as did tropical and sub-tropical Asia.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是气候变化温和且动物群保持完整的洲。

定位关键信息：根据题目的关键词"climatic change was mild"和"fauna remains"定位到F段，找到描述气候变化温和且动物群保持完整的句子。

解题思路：原文中明确提到"Significantly, Africa escaped major faunal extinction as did tropical and sub-tropical Asia"，说明非洲和热带及处于亚热带地区的亚洲的气候变化较为温和，且动物群得以保持完整。因此，答案为D。
38

38 the continents where both humans and climatic change are the causes
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Again, as animals come through to the last part of the cold stage, here there’s a fundamental change in the climate, reorganization of vegetation, and the combination of the climatic change and the presence of humans – of advanced Paleolithic humans – causes this wave of extinction. 

对应段落：G段  

审题：题目要求从四个大洲中选择一个，该大洲的巨型动物灭绝被证实是由人类和气候变化共同导致的。  

定位关键信息： 使用 "humans and climatic change are the causes" 作为关键词，定位文中明确将两者共同作用列为灭绝原因的段落。

解题思路：题目要求找出一个具体的大洲，其巨型动物灭绝是由“人类和气候变化共同造成的”。在段落G中，科学家A.J. Stuart的研究团队专注于“northern Eurasia”（北欧亚大陆，即欧洲加上西伯利亚）。该段明确指出，在最后一次冰期阶段末期，发生了根本性的气候变化和植被重组，而“气候变化和人类（先进的旧石器时代人类）的共同作用，导致了这波灭绝浪潮”。这直接对应了题目中“人类和气候变化都是原因”的描述。因此，对应的大洲是“Eurasia”，答案为A。
39

39 the continents where the climatic change along caused a massive extinction
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：It is believed that megafauna initially came into existence in response to glacial conditions and became extinct with the onset of warmer climates. Tropical and subtropical areas have experienced less radical climatic change. Significantly, Africa escaped major faunal extinction as did tropical and sub-tropical Asia.

对应段落：F段  

审题：题目问的是哪些大陆仅因气候变化导致了大规模灭绝。  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"climatic change"和"massive extinction"定位到F段，该段提到大型动物群（megafauna）的灭绝与气候变化有关。  

解题思路：文章前半部分提供了一个具体案例：爱尔兰麋鹿最初出现在欧洲和中亚地区，的最后灭绝地点是在西西伯利亚，西西伯利亚在地理上属于亚洲。段落F在总结气候变化时，明确将亚洲（与非洲一起）归类，没有大规模的动物群灭绝，并没有到亚洲地区出现的灭绝现象是由人为或者除气候以外的其他因素造成，这强烈暗示在作者看来，亚洲的灭绝主因或唯一原因被认为是气候变化。因此，选C。

Question 40 


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C, D. Write your answer in box 40 on your answer sheet.


40

Which statement is true according to the Stuart team’s finding?

正确答案： C. Climatic change was not solely responsible for the megafauna extinction in Europe.

原文答案句：There’s no obvious coincidence between the arrival of humans or climatic change alone and these extinctions. There’s a climatic change here, so there’s a double effect here. Again, as animals come through to the last part of the cold stage, here there’s a fundamental change in the climate, reorganization of vegetation, and the combination of the climatic change and the presence of humans – of advanced Paleolithic humans – causes this wave of extinction.  

对应段落：G段  

审题：题目问根据Stuart团队的研究发现，哪个陈述是正确的。  

定位关键信息：题目中关键词"Stuart team’s finding"指向G段，因为G段明确提到"the group of scientists led by A.J. Stuart"。  

解题思路：G段中Stuart团队的研究指出，欧洲大型动物灭绝的原因是"climatic change"和"the presence of humans"的双重作用，而非单一因素（"no obvious coincidence between...climatic change alone and these extinctions"）。选项C"climatic change was not solely responsible for the megafauna extinction in Europe"与此完全一致，说明气候变化并非唯一原因。其他选项均与原文表述不符：A项未提及尼安德特人的作用；B项错误，原文强调人类和气候变化的共同作用；D项将灭绝归因于气候变化，与原文的"双重因素"矛盾。因此C正确。
